refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these functions are separated. This refined FR focuses solely on the derivation aspect, which is a distinct functional requirement in the problem domain without implying any implementation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can analyze worksite mission context inputs and produce a documented set of performance requirements such as cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability, without specifying how this is done.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements.
The NGHE solution shall manage the history of performance requirements including baselines and calibration updates.

candidate_dps: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance requirements
Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets
Implement a version-controlled repository to maintain history of baselines and calibration updates
Incorporate safety and operational policy constraints into performance derivation logic
selected_dp.selected_dp: Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets

potential_sources: Tight coupling between worksite input changes and performance target updates
Interdependency between safety policies and performance derivation logic
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates the mapping framework from other system functions, supporting independence. Safety and policy constraints can be integrated as parameters without tightly coupling the core mapping logic.

assumptions_risks: Assumes worksite inputs are well-defined and consistently formatted
Risk that changes in safety policies may require updates to mapping framework
Maintaining traceability may increase complexity of data management
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope of worksite inputs and ensure modular interfaces for safety and policy constraints to minimize coupling and facilitate updates.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for integrating and managing data flows and performance metrics across the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data from vehicle and worksite sensors

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational commands and worksite input parameters

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts related to performance deviations or safety concerns

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication with external systems and modules

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as it centralizes data mapping and performance tracking; minimal diffusion since it interfaces with other modules via defined interfaces.
Contains some performance data but mapping framework would be spread across vehicle subsystems, causing diffusion.
Focuses on remote control rather than data mapping; would require extensive diffusion to implement DP.
Monitors safety-related performance but not designed for comprehensive mapping framework; moderate diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the traceable mapping framework because it is designed to integrate and manage system-wide data and performance targets, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause interface complexity.
If NGHE Solution grows too broad, it may become a bottleneck or single point of failure.
Changes in external interfaces (e.g., Telemetry format) could impact containment stability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision recommended; the selected DP aligns well with the NGHE Solution module, which provides a specific and cohesive boundary for implementation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP clearly addresses the FR by establishing a traceable mapping framework that links worksite inputs to performance targets, fulfilling the requirement to derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause interface complexity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and low diffusion scores, centralizing data mapping and performance tracking with well-defined external interfaces, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintaining history of baselines/calibration updates as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The NGHE Solution module is clearly defined as the implementation boundary responsible for integrating and managing data flows and performance metrics, providing clear allocation of the DP.

recommended_revisions: No revision recommended; the selected DP aligns well with the NGHE Solution module, which provides a specific and cohesive boundary for implementation.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear traceability, feasible verification, and well-defined implementation boundaries. The DP addresses the FR effectively, and the IM module centralizes functionality with low diffusion risk. Although there is a noted potential interface complexity risk due to dependencies, it is manageable and does not warrant further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and cohesive module boundary is preferable to avoid unnecessary complexity.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module will maintain high cohesion and low diffusion as designed.
Dependencies on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules will be managed to mitigate interface complexity risks.
open_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules causing interface complexity.
evidence_needed: Verification documentation showing traceability of performance targets to worksite inputs.
Records of baseline and calibration updates as per acceptance criteria.
